Title: Characterization of SOD and GPX Gene Families in the Soybeans in Response to Drought and Salinity Stresses

Plant_Genes.Summary_key_Findings_of_Core_Gene:
1. Identified as a member of the soybean SOD gene family through genome-wide analysis.
2. Root expression is markedly induced by oxidative stress (H2O2), with higher transcript levels at 3–12 h compared with the untreated control.
3. Shows continuous upregulation under salinity (250 mM NaCl), reaching maximal expression at 12 h after treatment.
4. The stress-inducible expression pattern supports its role as part of the ROS-scavenging response to abiotic stress in cultivated soybean.
Plant_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq expression profiling of soybean SOD genes across 27 tissues from SoyBase, complemented by gene-specific qRT-PCR validation under stress; public RNA-seq data used, accession not specified
Plant_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Displays robust, time-dependent transcriptional induction in roots under oxidative and salt stress, indicating a stress-responsive SOD component of the antioxidant defense in cultivated soybean
Plant_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Reactive oxygen species (ROS) detoxification / antioxidant defense (dismutation of O2− to H2O2 and O2)
Plant_Genes.Expression_Pattern: In roots, strongly upregulated by H2O2 and NaCl; expression increased at 3, 6 and 12 h after oxidative and salt treatments compared with 0 h control and remained continuously higher under NaCl, with maximum level at 12 h
Plant_Genes.Key_Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Oxidative stress (10 mM H2O2), salinity stress (250 mM NaCl) applied to soybean seedlings in Hoagland solution; roots sampled at 0, 3, 6, 12 h
Plant_Genes.Research_Topic: Abiotic stress response and antioxidant defense in soybean
Plant_Genes.Trait_Category: Abiotic stress (salinity and oxidative stress response)
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Methods: Stress treatments with 10 mM H2O2 spray and 250 mM NaCl in Hoagland solution; root sampling at 0–12 h; RNA isolation; cDNA synthesis; qRT-PCR with GsoActin-11 internal control; 2^-ΔΔCt analysis
Plant_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR expression analysis of roots under H2O2 and NaCl stress treatments
Plant_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Genome-wide identification of SOD family members followed by selection of representative genes for expression analysis
Plant_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Stress-inducible antioxidant gene in cultivated soybean; a candidate target for marker-assisted selection or transgenic approaches aiming to enhance tolerance to salinity and oxidative stress by reinforcing ROS-scavenging capacity in roots

Plant_Genes.Summary_key_Findings_of_Core_Gene:
1. Identified as a member of the wild soybean SOD gene family in the genome-wide survey.
2. Root expression is clearly induced by oxidative stress (H2O2), with higher transcript levels at 3–12 h relative to the untreated control.
3. Under salinity stress (250 mM NaCl), shows continuous upregulation throughout the 0–12 h time course, with maximum expression at 12 h.
4. The observed stress-responsive expression indicates that GsoSOD11.1 participates in the ROS-scavenging response of wild soybean to oxidative and salt stress.
Plant_Genes.Omics_Data: Included in the genome-wide SOD catalog for G. soja; expression characterized by qRT-PCR under stress; family-wide analyses (phylogeny, motifs, promoters) performed bioinformatically
Plant_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Acts as a stress-responsive SOD gene in wild soybean, with robust transcriptional induction in roots under oxidative and salinity stress conditions
Plant_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Reactive oxygen species (ROS) detoxification / antioxidant defense (dismutation of O2− to H2O2 and O2)
Plant_Genes.Expression_Pattern: In wild soybean roots, strongly upregulated by H2O2 and NaCl; expression is higher at 3, 6 and 12 h of oxidative and salt treatment than at 0 h and shows continuous induction under NaCl with peak expression at 12 h
Plant_Genes.Key_Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Oxidative stress (10 mM H2O2) and salinity (250 mM NaCl) imposed on hydroponically grown wild soybean seedlings; roots sampled over a 0–12 h time course
Plant_Genes.Research_Topic: Abiotic stress response and antioxidant defense in wild soybean
Plant_Genes.Trait_Category: Abiotic stress (salinity and oxidative stress response)
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Materials: Wild soybean (Glycine soja) genotype W05 seedlings grown in soil:vermiculite (1:1), then transferred to Hoagland solution; roots collected for analysis
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Methods: Growth of G. soja genotype W05 seedlings; application of 10 mM H2O2 and 250 mM NaCl in Hoagland solution; root sampling at 0, 3, 6, 12 h; RNA extraction; cDNA synthesis; qRT-PCR with GsoActin-11 internal control; relative expression calculated by 2^-ΔΔCt
Plant_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR expression analysis of wild soybean roots under H2O2 and NaCl stress treatments
Plant_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Genome-wide identification of SOD family members in Glycine soja followed by selection of representative genes for expression assays
Plant_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: A stress-inducible antioxidant gene from wild soybean that could be exploited as a genetic resource for improving salt and oxidative stress tolerance in cultivated soybean through introgression or transgenic approaches

Plant_Genes.Summary_key_Findings_of_Core_Gene:
1. Identified as part of the Glycine soja SOD gene family through genome-wide analysis.
2. Exhibits continuous transcriptional upregulation in roots exposed to PEG 6000, indicating responsiveness to osmotic/drought stress.
3. Expression remains higher than the 0 h control over the examined time points under PEG treatment.
4. The drought-inducible expression pattern suggests GsoSOD20.1 contributes to the antioxidant response of wild soybean under water-deficit stress.
Plant_Genes.Omics_Data: Included in the G. soja SOD gene set subjected to family-wide phylogenetic, structural and promoter analyses; gene-specific drought responsiveness characterized by qRT-PCR
Plant_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Functions as a PEG-inducible SOD gene in wild soybean roots, exhibiting sustained transcriptional activation under simulated drought stress
Plant_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Reactive oxygen species (ROS) detoxification / antioxidant defense (dismutation of O2− to H2O2 and O2)
Plant_Genes.Expression_Pattern: In wild soybean roots, shows continuous upregulation under PEG-induced drought stress relative to 0 h control, with sustained higher expression across the measured time points
Plant_Genes.Key_Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Osmotic/drought stress induced by 15% PEG 6000 in half-strength Hoagland solution, with roots sampled over a 0–12 h time course
Plant_Genes.Research_Topic: Drought stress response and antioxidant defense in wild soybean
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Materials: Wild soybean (Glycine soja) genotype W05 seedlings grown in controlled conditions and transferred to Hoagland solution; roots collected for stress-response analysis
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Methods: Application of 15% PEG 6000 in Hoagland solution to G. soja seedlings; root sampling at multiple time points (0–12 h); RNA extraction; cDNA synthesis; qRT-PCR using GsoActin-11 as internal control; 2^-ΔΔCt analysis
Plant_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR analysis of root expression under PEG 6000-induced drought stress
Plant_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Genome-wide identification of SOD genes in Glycine soja followed by selection of representative loci for stress-induced expression assays
Plant_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: A drought-inducible antioxidant gene from wild soybean that can serve as a promising resource for breeding or engineering soybean genotypes with improved tolerance to water-deficit conditions

Plant_Genes.Summary_key_Findings_of_Core_Gene:
1. Identified among Glycine soja GPX family members in a genome-wide survey.
2. Displays continuous transcriptional upregulation in roots at 3–9 h of H2O2 treatment relative to 0 h, indicating strong oxidative stress responsiveness.
3. Under PEG 6000-induced osmotic stress, expression at 12 h is higher than at 0 h, showing induction by drought-like conditions.
4. The combined oxidative and osmotic stress responsiveness supports its role in the ROS-scavenging response of wild soybean under multiple abiotic stresses.
Plant_Genes.Omics_Data: Part of the GPX gene family subjected to phylogenetic, structural and promoter analyses; specific gene expression dynamics characterized by qRT-PCR under oxidative and PEG-induced stress
Plant_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Behaves as a strongly stress-inducible GPX gene in wild soybean roots, with sustained transcriptional activation under oxidative and osmotic stress conditions
Plant_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Reactive oxygen species (ROS) detoxification via reduction of H2O2 and organic hydroperoxides using thiol electron donors
Plant_Genes.Expression_Pattern: Under H2O2 treatment, shows continuous upregulation at 3, 6 and 9 h relative to 0 h control; under PEG treatment, expression at 12 h is higher than at 0 h, indicating induction by drought/osmotic stress
Plant_Genes.Key_Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Oxidative stress (10 mM H2O2) and PEG 6000-induced osmotic stress applied to wild soybean seedlings; roots monitored over 0–12 h
Plant_Genes.Research_Topic: Abiotic stress-responsive antioxidant defense in wild soybean
Plant_Genes.Trait_Category: Abiotic stress (oxidative and drought/osmotic stress response)
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Materials: Wild soybean (Glycine soja) genotype W05 seedlings grown under controlled conditions and transferred to Hoagland solution; roots used for stress-induced expression analysis
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Methods: Imposition of oxidative stress by 10 mM H2O2 spray and osmotic stress by 15% PEG 6000 in Hoagland solution; root sampling at 0, 3, 6, 9 or 12 h; RNA isolation; cDNA synthesis; qRT-PCR with actin control and 2^-ΔΔCt quantification
Plant_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR time-course analysis of root expression under H2O2 and PEG 6000 treatments
Plant_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Genome-wide identification of GPX family members in Glycine soja with subsequent selection of representative loci for expression studies
Plant_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: A dual oxidative- and drought-inducible GPX gene from wild soybean, representing a promising candidate for enhancing multi-stress tolerance in soybean breeding or genetic engineering aimed at reinforcing ROS-scavenging capacity

Plant_Genes.Summary_key_Findings_of_Core_Gene:
1. Identified as a GPX family member in Glycine soja via genome-wide analysis.
2. Root expression is continuously elevated following 250 mM NaCl treatment relative to 0 h, with the highest level at 12 h.
3. The NaCl-inducible expression indicates involvement in the antioxidant response of wild soybean to salinity stress.
4. Provides a candidate GPX gene for improving salt stress tolerance in soybean through exploitation of wild germplasm.
Plant_Genes.Omics_Data: Member of the G. soja GPX gene family analyzed for phylogeny, structure and promoters; NaCl-responsive expression characterized by qRT-PCR
Plant_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Acts as a NaCl-inducible GPX gene in wild soybean roots, showing sustained transcriptional activation under salinity stress
Plant_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: ROS detoxification pathway, reducing H2O2 and hydroperoxides using thioredoxin or related electron donors
Plant_Genes.Expression_Pattern: In wild soybean roots, expression is continuously upregulated by NaCl treatment compared with 0 h control, with maximum transcript accumulation at 12 h
Plant_Genes.Key_Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Salinity stress imposed by 250 mM NaCl in nutrient solution; root tissues analyzed over a 0–12 h time course
Plant_Genes.Research_Topic: Salinity stress-responsive antioxidant defense in wild soybean
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Materials: Wild soybean (Glycine soja) genotype W05 seedlings grown under controlled conditions; roots used for NaCl stress-expression analysis
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Methods: Exposure of G. soja seedlings to 250 mM NaCl in Hoagland solution; root sampling at 0–12 h; RNA isolation; cDNA synthesis; qRT-PCR with actin internal control; relative expression determined by 2^-ΔΔCt
Plant_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR analysis of root expression under NaCl treatment
Plant_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Identified as part of the Glycine soja GPX gene family and selected for functional expression assay under NaCl stress
Plant_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: A salinity-inducible GPX gene from wild soybean that may be utilized as a donor locus or engineering target to improve salt tolerance in cultivated soybean by enhancing ROS-scavenging under high salinity

Plant_Genes.Summary_key_Findings_of_Core_Gene:
1. Identified as a GPX family member in Glycine soja through genome-wide analysis.
2. Displays increased root expression under PEG 6000 treatment, with higher transcript abundance at 12 h relative to 0 h.
3. The PEG-inducible pattern indicates involvement in the antioxidant response of wild soybean to osmotic/drought stress.
4. Represents a drought-responsive GPX candidate from wild germplasm for improving stress tolerance in soybean.
Plant_Genes.Omics_Data: Included in the Glycine soja GPX family subjected to phylogenetic, structural and promoter analyses; drought/osmotic stress responsiveness characterized by qRT-PCR
Plant_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Functions as a PEG-inducible GPX gene in wild soybean roots, showing enhanced transcription under simulated drought conditions
Plant_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: ROS detoxification, reducing H2O2 and lipid hydroperoxides via thiol-based peroxidase activity
Plant_Genes.Expression_Pattern: Under PEG treatment, expression in roots is higher at 12 h than at 0 h, indicating induction by osmotic/drought stress
Plant_Genes.Key_Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Osmotic/drought stress applied as 15% PEG 6000 in half-strength Hoagland solution; time-course sampling of roots (0–12 h)
Plant_Genes.Research_Topic: Drought/osmotic stress-responsive antioxidant defense in wild soybean
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Materials: Wild soybean (Glycine soja) genotype W05 seedlings; roots used for PEG-induced stress expression analysis
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Methods: Treatment of G. soja seedlings with 15% PEG 6000; root sampling at 0–12 h; RNA isolation; cDNA synthesis; qRT-PCR using actin internal control; 2^-ΔΔCt quantification
Plant_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR expression profiling of roots under PEG 6000-induced osmotic stress
Plant_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Identified among Glycine soja GPX genes in a genome-wide family survey and selected for PEG stress expression analysis
Plant_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: A PEG-inducible GPX gene from wild soybean that can be considered in breeding or engineering strategies aimed at improving drought tolerance by bolstering ROS detoxification in roots

Plant_Genes.Summary_key_Findings_of_Core_Gene:
1. Identified as a member of the Glycine max GPX gene family through genome-wide analysis.
2. Root expression is continuously elevated under 250 mM NaCl compared with 0 h, peaking at 12 h after treatment.
3. The NaCl-inducible expression pattern supports a role in the antioxidant response of cultivated soybean to salinity.
4. Represents a GPX candidate for manipulating salt stress responses in soybean improvement programs.
Plant_Genes.Omics_Data: Part of the Glycine max GPX family subjected to phylogenetic, structural and promoter analyses; NaCl-responsive expression characterized by qRT-PCR
Plant_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Acts as a NaCl-inducible GPX gene in cultivated soybean roots, with sustained transcriptional activation under salt stress
Plant_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: ROS detoxification pathway, catalyzing reduction of H2O2 and other hydroperoxides
Plant_Genes.Expression_Pattern: In roots, shows continuous upregulation under NaCl treatment relative to 0 h, with maximal transcript accumulation at 12 h
Plant_Genes.Key_Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Salinity stress imposed by 250 mM NaCl; root tissues sampled from treated Glycine max seedlings over 0–12 h
Plant_Genes.Research_Topic: Salinity stress-responsive antioxidant defense in cultivated soybean
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Methods: NaCl treatment (250 mM) of soybean seedlings followed by time-course root sampling and qRT-PCR to monitor GPX gene expression
Plant_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR analysis of root expression under NaCl treatment
Plant_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Identified in the Glycine max GPX gene family via genome-wide survey and selected for NaCl stress expression profiling
Plant_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: A salt-inducible GPX gene in cultivated soybean that provides a candidate locus for breeding or engineering improved salinity tolerance by reinforcing ROS-scavenging in roots

Plant_Genes.Summary_key_Findings_of_Core_Gene:
1. Identified as a member of the GPX gene family in Glycine max.
2. Exhibits continuous upregulation in roots under PEG 6000 treatment compared with 0 h, indicating responsiveness to osmotic/drought stress.
3. The PEG-inducible expression suggests involvement in the antioxidant response of cultivated soybean under water-deficit conditions.
4. Serves as a candidate GPX gene for manipulating drought stress responses in soybean breeding and biotechnology.
Plant_Genes.Omics_Data: Included in the Glycine max GPX gene family; PEG-responsive expression characterized by qRT-PCR
Plant_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Functions as a PEG-inducible GPX gene in cultivated soybean roots, exhibiting sustained transcriptional activation under osmotic/drought stress
Plant_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: ROS detoxification via reduction of H2O2 and hydroperoxides
Plant_Genes.Expression_Pattern: In roots, shows continuous increase in expression under PEG treatment relative to 0 h control
Plant_Genes.Key_Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Osmotic/drought stress induced by 15% PEG 6000; roots sampled between 0 and 12 h after treatment
Plant_Genes.Research_Topic: Drought/osmotic stress-responsive antioxidant defense in cultivated soybean
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Methods: Treatment of cultivated soybean seedlings with 15% PEG 6000; time-course root sampling; RNA extraction; cDNA synthesis; qRT-PCR with actin internal control; relative expression calculated by 2^-ΔΔCt
Plant_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR analysis of root expression under PEG 6000 treatment
Plant_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Identified among Glycine max GPX family members and selected for PEG-induced stress expression analysis
Plant_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: A drought/osmotic stress-inducible GPX gene in cultivated soybean, providing a candidate for improving drought tolerance through enhanced ROS-scavenging capacity in root tissues
